data.title: Bloomberg analyst: Musk may use SPCX as SpaceX's IPO ticker symbol.
data.description: Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, stated on the X platform that Musk is likely to use SPCX as the ticker symbol for SpaceX's IPO. This symbol was previously held by Matt Tuttle, but the relevant ETFs have already changed their ticker symbols. Balchunas pointed out that the Bloomberg ETF team predicted this as early as last December, and the discovery of the SpaceX IPO ticker symbol "SPCX" was made by Will Hershey, co-founder of Roundhill Investments, who had previously "given" his META ticker to Mark Zuckerberg, calling it "a tacit understanding among peers."

data.title: On April 7th, US Bitcoin ETFs saw a net inflow of 6,665 BTC, and Ethereum ETFs saw a net inflow of 39,768 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, on April 7, the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 6,665 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 39,768 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 2,919 SOL.

data.title: BlackRock withdrew 2,607 BTC and 28,391 ETH from Coinbase Prime
data.description: According to Lookonchain, BlackRock just withdrew 2,607 BTC (worth $177.56 million) and 28,391 ETH (worth $59 million) from a Coinbase Prime address.

data.title: EIA Short-Term Energy Outlook Report: Middle East supply disruptions are expected to continue until the end of 2026.
data.description: The EIA's Short-Term Energy Outlook report indicates that Middle East supply disruptions are expected to continue until the end of 2026. Oil production cuts in the Middle East due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz are projected to increase to 9.1 million barrels per day in April. The Brent-WTI crude oil price spread is expected to peak at $15 per barrel in April, at which point Middle East oil supply disruptions will reach their maximum. US retail gasoline prices are projected to reach their highest average level since 2022 in 2026. Global oil demand is projected at 104.6 million barrels per day in 2026, lower than the previous forecast of 105.2 million barrels per day; demand is projected at 106.2 million barrels per day in 2027, also lower than the previous forecast of 106.6 million barrels per day. (Jinshi)

data.title: XRP Spot ETFs Experience Slowdown Amid Regulatory Uncertainty
data.description: XRP spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs) currently manage approximately $1 billion in assets. According to NS3.AI, inflows for 2026 have significantly decelerated, with March witnessing net outflows of $28 million. On Monday, XRP was trading at around $1.30, marking a decline of about 40% from its peak in January. A key factor to monitor is the upcoming CLARITY Act, which aims to establish a U.S. digital asset framework and could influence the resurgence of institutional demand.

data.title: EIA Revises U.S. Natural Gas Production Forecasts for 2026 and 2027
data.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) has updated its short-term energy outlook, adjusting its forecasts for U.S. natural gas production. According to Jin10, the EIA now projects that U.S. dry natural gas production will reach 1096.0 billion cubic feet per day in 2026, slightly up from the previous forecast of 1095.0 billion cubic feet per day. For 2027, the EIA anticipates production will increase to 1126.0 billion cubic feet per day, compared to the earlier estimate of 1123.0 billion cubic feet per day. These revisions reflect the EIA's ongoing assessment of market conditions and production capabilities.

data.title: A newly created wallet withdrew 300 BTC from Binance, worth $20.43 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created wallet just withdrew 300 BTC from Binance, worth $20.43 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Active Addresses Reach Lowest Level Since 2026
data.description:
Bitcoin's active addresses have dropped to their lowest point since 2026, marking an eight-year low, according to BlockBeats. Analyst Gaah suggests that the current market environment, characterized by low volatility and speculative activity, is ideal for 'smart money' and institutional investors to accumulate large positions without causing significant price fluctuations.

The decline in activity indicates that short-term investors, who typically buy on hype and sell in panic, have largely exited the market. The remaining participants are primarily long-term holders who are in the process of accumulating.

Historically, low points in active address metrics often align with the most profitable long-term bottoming phases. The current 'silence' in the network suggests that the available supply is being absorbed by the market.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 600 USDT with a Narrowed 1.23% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 07, 2026, 16:03 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 600 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 600.789978 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.23% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Robinhood CEO: Trump account will attract the next generation of investors
data.description: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev stated that the company's "Trump Accounts," launched in partnership with the U.S. Treasury Department, will allow Robinhood to reach the next generation of investors and drive future growth. In an interview with CNBC, Tenev said, "This will make Robinhood reach the next generation…for millions of children, this will be their first investment account." He emphasized that the company's short-term focus is on the U.S. government partnership, rather than directly profiting from the accounts, which will be free of fees and commissions. Previously, the U.S. Treasury Department announced that Robinhood and BNY Mellon were selected as partners to open tax-deferred escrow investment accounts for children born between 2025 and 2028. Robinhood will act as broker and custodian, working with BNY to provide the front-end application and customer support. As of March 31, more than 4 million children had registered for Trump Accounts, and over 1 million children were eligible for the Treasury Department's $1,000 start-up capital pilot program. Several financial institutions, including JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, SoFi, BlackRock, and Charles Schwab, will match the federal start-up funds for the children of their employees. (CNBC)

data.title: Analysis: The market is becoming less sensitive to Trump, and Bitcoin's implied volatility has decreased significantly compared to last week.
data.description: Greeks.live posted on the X platform that the market is becoming less sensitive to Trump, and Bitcoin's implied volatility has decreased significantly compared to last week. Although Trump will definitely make statements that affect the market, no matter what he says, even if he says he will send ground troops tomorrow, it will not deviate from market expectations. Currently, the changes in skew and trading volume distribution are quite scattered, and the market has no consensus and is waiting.

data.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman Accelerates IPO Plans Amid Financial Concerns
data.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is rapidly advancing plans for an initial public offering (IPO), despite internal warnings about significant cash consumption by 2030. According to Foresight News, Altman has committed to investing $600 billion in computing power. This move comes as OpenAI faces challenges related to its financial sustainability, with projections indicating a sharp increase in cash usage over the next decade.

data.title: Trump Confirms Planned Action Against Iran Amid Potential Negotiations
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the planned action against Iran is still scheduled for 8 PM. According to NS3.AI, Trump indicated that the decision could be altered if there is progress in negotiations on the same day.

data.title: PlanB Predicts Potential Short-Term Bitcoin Decline Before Bull Market
data.description: PlanB has suggested that Bitcoin might experience a short-term decline before transitioning into a bull market and reaching a new all-time high. According to NS3.AI, PlanB noted that the current phase could precede a market bottom, drawing comparisons with previous cycles.

data.title: Sam Altman continues to accelerate OpenAI's IPO and has pledged $600 billion for computing power.
data.description: Despite private warnings from OpenAI that its cash burn will increase significantly by 2030, Sam Altman is accelerating the IPO process and has pledged $600 billion to invest in computing power. (The Information)

data.title: Survey Reveals Shift from Crypto to Gold Among U.S. Investors
data.description:
A recent MarketWise survey involving 1,000 active investors in the United States has highlighted a notable shift in investment preferences. According to NS3.AI, the survey revealed that 18% of participants have either sold or reduced their cryptocurrency holdings over the past year in favor of purchasing gold. This trend underscores a growing inclination towards traditional safe-haven assets amid financial uncertainties.

The survey further indicated that in a financial emergency, 60% of the respondents would place their trust in gold, compared to only 13% who would choose Bitcoin. This preference suggests a cautious approach among investors, prioritizing stability and reliability in their asset choices.

data.title: World Bank President: Middle East Conflict May Lead to Inflation and Slower Economic Growth
data.description: World Bank President Ajay Banga has stated that the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is likely to contribute to rising inflation and a slowdown in economic growth. According to Jin10, Banga emphasized the potential economic repercussions of the conflict, highlighting concerns over increased inflationary pressures and a deceleration in global economic activity. The situation in the Middle East remains a significant factor influencing economic forecasts and market stability.

data.title: OPEC Oil Production Sees Historic Decline Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description:
OPEC's oil production experienced its largest decline in over four decades in March due to disruptions in exports from key member countries amid Middle East conflicts. According to Odaily, a survey revealed that OPEC's daily output plummeted by 7.56 million barrels, or approximately 25%, to 22 million barrels. This marks the most significant monthly drop since data compilation began in 1989, surpassing the 1973 Arab oil embargo in terms of barrel count.

Daniel Yergin's book, "The Prize," notes that during the 1973 crisis, the market faced a daily loss of 5 million barrels from October to December, although the global market was much smaller at that time. The survey indicated that Iraq, heavily reliant on the Strait of Hormuz, saw the largest production decrease among OPEC members, with a reduction of 2.76 million barrels per day to 1.63 million barrels. Saudi Arabia and the UAE followed, although they managed to partially reroute exports through alternative oil pipelines. Saudi Arabia's daily production fell by 2.07 million barrels to 8.36 million barrels, while the UAE's output decreased by 1.44 million barrels to 2.16 million barrels. Despite Saudi Arabia's ability to export via the Red Sea, tanker tracking data showed a roughly 50% decline in its exports in March.

data.title: Charles Schwab Study Highlights Impact of Small Bitcoin and Ethereum Allocations
data.description:
A recent study by Charles Schwab reveals that even a small allocation of 1% to 3% in Bitcoin (BTC) or Ethereum (ETH) can significantly affect the overall risk profile of an investment portfolio. According to ChainCatcher, the research indicates that both Bitcoin and Ethereum have historically experienced declines exceeding 70%, which is much higher than the volatility levels of stocks or bonds. As a result, even minor allocations can have a noticeable impact during periods of market fluctuation.

Charles Schwab suggests two methods for allocating crypto assets: the traditional portfolio theory approach and the risk budgeting method. The traditional approach involves allocation based on expected returns, volatility, and correlation. However, due to significant differences in return assumptions, even aggressive investors may find it challenging to justify large allocations if expected returns are below 10%. The risk budgeting method focuses on the level of risk an investor is willing to take, shifting the emphasis from returns to risk tolerance. Despite this, the volatility of crypto assets may still exceed expectations.

Charles Schwab emphasizes that crypto assets are highly volatile and may not be suitable for all investors. It advises investors to carefully consider their risk tolerance, investment horizon, and familiarity with the assets before making allocations. Additionally, investors should be mindful of risks related to liquidity, theft, and fraud.

data.title: Trump said he would consider changing the course of the strikes against Iran if negotiations made progress.
data.description: US President Trump stated that "the operation at 8 p.m. will still take place," and that an "unprecedented strike" against Iran is still possible, but he does not rule out changing his decision if negotiations progress that day. (CCTV News)
